claim: A single prompt hits a wall when solving complex problems; multi-agent frameworks solve this by having specialized agents collaborate on different subtasks
actionable: Split complex tasks into roles (engineer, analyst, reviewer) and assign different agents to each; this outperforms a single agent with a long prompt
source_title: One Prompt is NOT enough: Using AutoGen to code a Multi-Agent Postgres AI Tool

claim: AutoGen enables agents to work together by sending messages, running tools, and updating shared state—similar to how a real team coordinates
actionable: Use AutoGen to model real organizational workflows (data engineer → analyst → product manager) rather than linear prompt chains
source_title: One Prompt is NOT enough: Using AutoGen to code a Multi-Agent Postgres AI Tool

claim: Function mapping in AutoGen restricts which agent can execute which code; only agents with a function_map can run arbitrary tools
actionable: Assign function_map only to the agent that should execute SQL (e.g., analyst), not to planning agents, to control what each agent can do
source_title: One Prompt is NOT enough: Using AutoGen to code a Multi-Agent Postgres AI Tool

claim: Setting 'human_input_mode': 'NEVER' lets AutoGen agents run autonomously without asking for user confirmation on each step
actionable: Use 'human_input_mode': 'NEVER' when deploying agents in production; use 'TERMINATE' mode only for interactive debugging
source_title: One Prompt is NOT enough: Using AutoGen to code a Multi-Agent Postgres AI Tool

claim: Group chat termination in AutoGen requires a termination function that looks for specific keywords ('approved') in agent responses to signal completion
actionable: Design your agent prompts to always output a termination keyword (e.g., 'approved') when work is complete, not when intermediate steps finish
source_title: One Prompt is NOT enough: Using AutoGen to code a Multi-Agent Postgres AI Tool

claim: Multi-agent systems cost more and scale linearly with agent count due to LLM API calls and token usage for inter-agent communication
actionable: Monitor token usage in multi-agent systems and consider trade-offs: 3 agents may cost 3x more but produce 10x better results on complex tasks
source_title: One Prompt is NOT enough: Using AutoGen to code a Multi-Agent Postgres AI Tool

claim: Debugging multi-agent systems is harder than single-agent ones because agents communicate loosely; issues require tracing through multiple LLM calls
actionable: Log all inter-agent messages and tool outputs to a file; trace execution step-by-step to isolate where hallucinations or errors occur
source_title: One Prompt is NOT enough: Using AutoGen to code a Multi-Agent Postgres AI Tool

claim: One agent + one function is better than one agent with many functions; this mirrors Unix philosophy and improves model focus
actionable: Design agents to do one thing exceptionally well; give the data engineer SQL generation only, not validation or execution
source_title: One Prompt is NOT enough: Using AutoGen to code a Multi-Agent Postgres AI Tool
